title: clematis villosa subsp. kirkii
intro: Clematis villosa subsp. kirkii is a plant species belonging to the Clematis genus, commonly known as the hairy clematis or New Zealand clematis. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating rheumatic pain, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are administered to treat various ailments.
